news.headline: Cake secures $13MM in seed funding led by Gradient, aiming to democratize AI for mid-market businesses.
news.summary: Cake, a platform for managed open-source AI infrastructure, has revealed that it secured $13 million in funding to provide advanced AI technology to the mid-market.

news.headline: American stock futures and bond yields fall due to reports of Putin's updated nuclear doctrine.
Ancora supports Amcor's $8.4B acquisition of Berry, an activist investor report reveals.
Empire Continental Land rebrands to Precedent Land Co under Willowdale Asset Mgmt in Vaughan, ON. (Dec 02, 2024)
news.summary: On December 02, 2024, in Vaughan, Ontario, Empire Continental Land rebranded itself as Precedent Land Company. This division, previously part of Empire Communities, is now an independent entity under Willowdale Asset Management. The transition signifies a new chapter for the company as it establishes itself in the market.

news.headline: Trump chooses experienced individuals for key roles in his administration, avoiding unconventional picks.
news.summary: The President-elect, Donald Trump, has opted for traditional choices for his economic team, selecting individuals with experience in Wall Street or established Republicans for important positions in his administration.

news.headline: November jobs report shows U.S. added 227,000 jobs, but unemployment rate rises, prompting Fed rate cut hopes. S&P 500 gains.
news.summary: The November jobs report revealed that the United States saw an increase of 227,000 jobs, surpassing expectations. However, there was also a rise in the unemployment rate, leading to anticipations for a potential rate cut by the Federal Reserve. The news resulted in a positive response from the stock market, with the S&P 500 experiencing gains.

news.headline: Goldman Sachs predicts CRE services sector growth in 2025, recommends CBRE, Newmark, and Jones Lang LaSalle as top picks.
news.summary: Goldman Sachs is optimistic about the future of commercial real estate (CRE) services stocks, noting a positive trend in the sector after challenging years. The investment bank has highlighted CBRE Group Inc. (NYSE: CBRE), Newmark Group Inc. (NASDAQ: NMRK), and Jones Lang LaSalle Inc. (NYSE: JLL) as its top picks for 2025. According to Goldman Sachs analyst Julien Blouin, the CRE services sector has seen a resurgence in 2024, with property values stabilizing and improved financing conditions. Factors contributing to this growth include lower short-term rates, steadier long-term rates, and reduced credit spreads, creating a more robust environment for real estate transactions. Blouin also observed a notable increase in origination volumes across various property types and lender groups, indicating a more active market.

news.headline: SOLVD Health's Dr. Keri Donaldson will present at William Blair’s Innovator Series on Dec 17, 2024, from 1:00 to 1:45 p.m.
news.summary: San Diego-based patient intelligence company SOLVD Health revealed that its Founder and CEO, Dr. Keri Donaldson, is set to present a company update at William Blair’s Innovator Series: Life Science Tools and Diagnostics. The virtual fireside chat will occur on December 17, 2024, from 1:00 p.m. to 1:45 p.m. ET. SOLVD Health focuses on developing advanced precision solutions for better health outcomes. This event will serve as a platform for Dr. Donaldson to share insights and engage with the audience.

news.headline: Federal judge blocks Kroger’s $24.6B deal with Albertsons
Hologic responds to USPSTF's draft cervical cancer screening guidelines in Marlborough, Mass. $HOLX #USPSTF.
news.summary: Marlborough, MA - Hologic recently released a statement in response to the USPSTF's draft guidelines on cervical cancer screening. The guidelines recommend that women aged 21 to 65 undergo cervical cancer screening every three years, or every five years for women aged 30 to 65 who opt for HPV testing. Hologic emphasized the importance of regular screenings in detecting cervical cancer early and improving treatment outcomes. The company's commitment to women's health is underscored by its innovative technologies that support accurate and efficient screening processes.

news.headline: US stocks may open lower, Dow hit 45K. Futures show Nasdaq -0.03%, S&P 500 -0.08%, and Dow -0.04%. Bitcoin rally impacts S&P, Powell optimistic about labor market. Odds of Dec rate cut at 66.7%, up from last week.
news.summary: Investors may see U.S. stocks opening lower on Friday after the Dow broke 45,000 for the first time, with Futures of major indices slightly down signaling caution on Wall Street. Investors are awaiting crucial payroll data and monitoring Federal Reserve chair Powell's comments on the economy. Powell noted strong growth and rising inflation, but market expectations for a rate cut in December rose to 66.7%. In premarket trading, the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust fell 0.02% while Invesco QQQ ETF remained flat. Crude oil prices dropped after OPEC+ delayed production output increase. Treasury yields rose slightly. U.S. initial jobless claims also increased.

news.headline: US adds 227,000 jobs in Nov, wage growth speeds up, exceeding expectations. May impact interest rate cuts. Payrolls rose from 36,000 to 227,000.
news.summary: The latest report on November's job numbers showed a strong rebound in hiring, surpassing expectations. Nonfarm payrolls spiked by 227,000, a significant increase from the revised 36,000 in October. Wage growth also exceeded predictions. This robust labor market performance may shift market anticipations regarding a potential interest rate decrease, as the solid employment gains and growing wages could lower the pressure on policymakers to adjust monetary policy. The data revealed nonfarm payrolls rising well above the projected 200,000, as estimated by TradingEconomics. The employment trend exhibited a positive trajectory throughout the month. For further details, visit Benzinga.com.

news.headline: November jobs report shows nonfarm payrolls increased by 227,000, easing concerns of labor market softening after October. Experts suggest slower interest rate cuts in the future.
news.summary: The latest jobs report for November revealed a 227,000 increase in nonfarm payrolls, marking a substantial rise from the previous month, which was affected by weather issues and a Boeing strike. Analysts are now considering the implications of this report on future interest rates. Glen Smith, CIO of GDS Wealth Management, believes that the robust jobs data eases concerns about a weakening labor market and anticipates a potential interest rate cut by the Fed in December. However, he acknowledges the Fed's indication of a more patient approach towards rates in 2025. Jane Oates, ex-Assistant Labor Secretary and current senior policy advisor at WorkingNation, highlighted the significant job growth observed, with nearly half of the... (This text has been abridged. For the complete story, visit the original website).

news.headline: Dow Jones futures increase, led by Salesforce earnings. Nasdaq hits new high with Meta Platforms, Palantir, and Credo Tech.
news.summary: Stock index futures increased, with a notable rise in the market driven by strong earnings from Salesforce. On Tuesday, the Nasdaq achieved a record high, propelled by Meta Platforms, Palantir, and Credo Tech. Dow Jones futures also saw a boost as investors showed enthusiasm towards the market. Nvidia showed strength by entering the buy zone, bringing positive sentiment in the stock market.

news.headline: Biden imposes stricter trade restrictions on China's chip industry, criticized for industry-friendly exceptions.
news.summary: The updated regulations introduce a notable increase in limitations for the sale of chip technology to China. However, critics argue that there are exemptions included in the rules that benefit the industry. The new restrictions could have a profound impact on the tech market, as they aim to control the transfer of sensitive technology to China. These regulations are part of a broader effort to protect intellectual property and national security. The rules come at a time when tensions between nations are high, with the US imposing stringent measures to restrict the transfer of technology to China. The carve-outs in the restrictions are causing concern among some experts as they fear that they could weaken the intended impact of the rules. Despite these criticisms, the regulations mark a significant shift in the approach to controlling the export of chip technology, with the potential to alter the landscape of the industry. It is essential for companies to closely examine the implications of these rules on their operations and strategies moving forward.

news.headline: Dow Jones up 280 pts post surprise jobs report; Fed's Powell to speak next. Salesforce grows on earnings.
news.summary: On Wednesday, the Dow Jones experienced a significant increase of 280 points following an unexpected positive ADP jobs report. The next event on the horizon is a speech by Fed chief Powell. Additionally, Salesforce saw a substantial rise in its stock value due to strong earnings. It was a day marked by notable financial movements in the market.

news.headline: 227,000 jobs added in November, but unemployment rate also sees an increase.
Dow Jones Futures near highs, Bitcoin below $100K, jobs report imminent. Advice on when to sell "heat" stocks.
news.summary: As the market reaches close to its peak, investors eagerly await the upcoming jobs report. Bitcoin experienced a drop below $100,000, causing concern. The question arises: When is the right time for investors to consider selling "heat" stocks?

news.headline: FRED, the data tool of St. Louis Fed, has captured America's attention with its widespread use in Facebook debates and college classes.
news.summary: The data tool created by the St. Louis Federal Reserve has become widely popular, being utilized in discussions ranging from Facebook political debates to university lectures.

news.headline: Tech stocks, Meta rises as court backs TikTok ban, Bitcoin hits $101,000. S&P 500/Nasdaq 100 set 3rd week gains. Meta up 9%, Nasdaq 100 best week in month, Dow down, Meta rallying on TikTok ban upheld, Amazon, Tesla lead consumer discretionary sector.
news.summary: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 are heading for their third straight week of gains, with the Nasdaq 100 showing its strongest weekly performance in a month. This reflects investor interest in tech stocks and the increasing possibility of lower interest rates. On the other hand, the Dow Jones is set to finish the week in negative territory, showcasing a variance in performance among key U.S. indices. Meta Platforms Inc. (NASDAQ:META) saw its shares climb 3% on Wednesday, leading to a weekly gain of over 9%, making it the stock's best week since late January. This increase came after a U.S. appeals court upheld the Jan. 19 deadline for ByteDance, the parent company of TikTok, to divest the app due to national security and data privacy concerns. TikTok's appeal was turned down, with the U.S. Supreme Court remaining as its final option. The consumer discretionary sector performed well on Friday, driven by Amazon.com Inc. (NASDAQ:AMZN) and Tesla Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA). For further details, visit Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Dow Jones up on good jobs report, Lululemon stock jumps 16% on earnings.
news.summary: The Dow Jones experienced an increase on Friday following a November job report that exceeded expectations. Lululemon stock witnessed a substantial surge of 16% due to strong earnings. This positive news contributed to a favorable trading day for investors.

news.headline: Post-election poll shows shift in views among Trump voters. POLITICO|Morning Consult polls highlight changing opinions.
news.summary: Two recent surveys conducted by POLITICO|Morning Consult provide insight into shifting public opinion surrounding the election. The polls were conducted during the final days of the election and after Trump's victory, revealing significant changes in sentiments among the population.

news.headline: $20B loan backed by Russian assets transferred to Ukraine to avoid economic collapse after Trump's inauguration.
news.summary: Following President-elect Donald J. Trump's inauguration, Ukraine will receive essential economic assistance to bolster its financial stability. This lifeline will provide significant financial support to Ukraine during this crucial time.

news.headline: MARA, a US Bitcoin miner, acquires $1.1B in BTC, adding 11,774 coins to its total of almost $4B.
news.summary: On Tuesday, MARA, an American Bitcoin mining company, revealed its acquisition of 11,774 BTC for $1.1 billion. This purchase has elevated their total BTC holdings to nearly $4 billion.

news.headline: Job seekers are facing challenges in finding employment despite an increase of 5.4% in job openings.
news.summary: A recent report indicates that the COVID-19 pandemic has caused a total of 5.3 million infections and 190,000 deaths worldwide. The United States accounts for the highest number of cases, with 1.2 million infections and 65,000 deaths. Additionally, the global economy is expected to shrink by 3% due to the impact of the pandemic, leading to predictions of a 5% decline in global trade. The report also highlights the challenges facing healthcare systems, as hospitals are overwhelmed and facing shortages of medical supplies and personnel. International efforts are being made to develop a vaccine, with 115 projects currently in progress. The pandemic has also brought about significant changes in the way people work and interact, with remote working becoming the new norm for many industries. The report emphasizes the importance of implementing safety measures and following guidelines to prevent further spread of the virus.

news.headline: Trump admin faces challenges in removing NLRB members, including logistical issues. Former officials and attorneys weigh in.
news.summary: Former officials and lawyers representing federal employees say that the Trump administration could face significant obstacles if they try to oust members of the National Labor Relations Board.

news.headline: Biden criticizes Trump's tax and tariffs plans, calling them a significant error.
news.summary: In a recent development, it has been reported that the unemployment rate in the country has reached an all-time low of 3.7%. This decrease represents a significant improvement from the previous rate of 4.2%. The number of jobs added in the last month alone was 250,000, contributing to this positive trend in the labor market. Additionally, the GDP growth rate for the last quarter was recorded at 5.2%, surpassing expectations and indicating a strong economic performance. Experts have pointed to various factors, such as increased consumer spending and business investments, as reasons for this growth. Overall, these indicators suggest a robust and thriving economy in the country.

news.headline: Liberty Global to reveal full-year 2024 results on Feb 18, 2025, with Investor Call scheduled for Feb 19, 2025 at 9:00 a.m. ET.
news.summary: Denver, Colorado-based Liberty Global Ltd. (NASDAQ: LBTYA, LBTYB, LBTYK) has revealed its intention to unveil its complete financial outcomes for the fiscal year 2024 on Tuesday, February 18, 2025. Interested parties are encouraged to participate in the Investor Call scheduled for 09:00 a.m. (Eastern Time) on Wednesday, February 19, 2025. The management will review the company's performance and potentially disclose future projections during the call. An exclusive listen-only webcast will accompany the event.

news.headline: Nihar Gala introduces Nihar Gala Grant for Healthcare Students, supporting undergraduate students in healthcare.
news.summary: On December 10, 2024, in Millsboro, Delaware, Nihar Gala, the founder and CEO of Alpha Care Medical, announced the launch of the Nihar Gala Grant for Healthcare Students. This grant is designed to support the education of future healthcare professionals by offering financial assistance.

news.headline: Frequency Electronics, Inc. reports $15.8M & $30.9M revenues for Q2 & FY 2025, $2.6M & $5.0M operating income, $2.7M & $5.1M net income.
news.summary: Mitchel Field, N.Y., Dec. 10, 2024 - Frequency Electronics, Inc. (NASDAQ-FEIM) announced its financial results for the three and six months ended October 31, 2024. Revenues reached approximately $15.8 million and $30.9 million, respectively, showing growth compared to $13.6 million and $26.0 million from the same period in the previous fiscal year. Operating income for these periods was $2.6 million and $5.0 million, a significant increase from $0.9 million and $3.0 million in the prior year. Net income from operations for the three and six months ended October 31, 2024 stood at $2.7 million ($0.28 per diluted share) and $5.1 million ($0.53 per diluted share), showing improvement compared to $0.8 million ($0.08 per diluted share) and $2.9 million ($0.30 per diluted share) for the same periods in 2023.

news.headline: NIH's NIAID announced STOMP study interim results on tecovirimat: no efficacy in Mpox lesion resolution, stopping patient enrollment.
news.summary: In New York on December 10, 2024, the National Institutes of Health’s (NIH) National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases (NIAID) revealed findings from an interim examination of the Study of Tecovirimat for Human Mpox Virus (STOMP) clinical trial (NCT05534984). NIAID disclosed that SIGA’s tecovirimat, an antiviral therapy, did not exhibit effectiveness in promptly resolving skin and mucosal lesions in patients with mild to moderate clade II mpox in comparison to a placebo. Following this outcome and further evaluations, the study Data Safety and Monitoring Board (DSMB) recommended cessation of patient enrolment in the randomized study arms. NIAID accepted this suggestion and also decided to halt enrolment in the open label arm of the study, which involved patients with severe conditions or those at risk of developing severe disease. Detailed analysis is still pending for primary endpoint subcategories and specific secondary and exploratory outcomes.

news.headline: Atlantic International Corp. will start trading on Nasdaq on Dec. 11, 2024, under ticker symbol "ATLN." No action needed from shareholders.
news.summary: Atlantic International Corp., a prominent provider of strategic staffing, outsourced services, and workforce solutions, shared that they have been given the green light to list their common stock on the Nasdaq Global Market. This shift is scheduled to take place on December 11, 2024, with trading under the symbol “ATLN”. Previously, the company's shares were traded on the OTC Markets. Shareholders are advised that no action is needed in response to this uplisting.

news.headline: U.S. could see interest rate cut in December. Markets predict an 86% chance for a 25-basis-point cut, lowering the rate to 4.25%-4.5%. November CPI expected to rise to 2.7%, a potential boost for year-end markets.
news.summary: U.S. policymakers are facing a crucial decision on whether to cut interest rates, with the November Consumer Price Index release scheduled for Wednesday at 8:30 a.m. ET potentially influencing the outcome of the Federal Reserve’s upcoming Dec. 18 meeting. Market expectations currently indicate an 86% likelihood of a 25-basis-point rate cut, which would bring the federal funds rate down to a range of 4.25%-4.5%. The upcoming inflation data will play a significant role, as it could either bolster year-end market performance or complicate the chances of a Santa Claus Rally. Economists are predicting a rise in annual CPI inflation from 2.6% in October to 2.7% in November, marking a continued upward trend. Additionally, monthly CPI growth is anticipated to be 0.3%, higher than the 0.2% increases seen in recent reports. The Core CPI figure is also a key metric to watch.

news.headline: Zefiro joins Alberta working group on oil & gas wells for proper retirement & management; collaboration with stakeholders.
news.summary: Zefiro has joined the "Mature Asset Strategy Working Group" under the Drilling and Completion Committee in Alberta. This membership involves collaborating with various stakeholders from public, private, and non-profit sectors to advance the closure of orphaned wells and ensure the effective management of marginal wells in the province. This initiative aims to address the significant issue of abandoned wells in Alberta, with over [important number] orphaned wells requiring retirement and proper maintenance. Through this partnership, Zefiro is committed to promoting environmental sustainability and regulatory compliance within the oil and gas sector.

news.headline: 90% manufacturers prioritize regionalization for supply chain robustness; 2/3 opt for "power-of-two" strategy. FDI appeal wanes in India, Brazil by 15%.
news.summary: Around 66% of manufacturers are implementing a "power-of-two" approach by directing most of their direct spending towards two distinct regions. The appeal of low-cost manufacturing centers like India and Brazil for Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) declines by 15%. This trend was reported in a press release dated December 10, 2024, from London.

news.headline: HealthWell Foundation ranked #24 on Forbes' 2024 list of America's top 100 charities for its 100% fundraising efficiency.
news.summary: The HealthWell Foundation®, a nonprofit based in Germantown, Maryland, was recently acknowledged for achieving a 100% fundraising efficiency. The foundation, known for offering financial aid to underinsured individuals in the United States, has been ranked as the 24th organization in the 2024 report. This recognition indicates the organization's commitment to utilizing all funds effectively to support those in need.

news.headline: Marvell unveils 1.6 Tbps LPO chipset for optical fabric interconnects, overcoming copper cable limitations.
news.summary: Marvell Technology, Inc., a prominent company specializing in data infrastructure semiconductor solutions, has recently unveiled the general release of a 200G product. This groundbreaking development aims to overcome the speed and reach restrictions commonly associated with passive, direct-attached copper (DAC) cables. The announcement was made on December 10, 2024, from Santa Clara, California. Marvell's continuous commitment to innovation in the industry is evident through this significant release.

news.headline: Marvell's new custom HBM Compute Architecture boosts cloud AI accelerators: 25% more compute, 33% greater memory, better efficiency. Collaboration with Micron, Samsung, SK hynix for custom XPUs.
news.summary: Marvell has launched a cutting-edge AI accelerator architecture, known as XPU, that boasts significant improvements in performance and efficiency. The architecture allows for up to 25% more computing power and 33% increased memory capacity while enhancing power efficiency. Marvell is working closely with industry giants Micron, Samsung, and SK hynix to develop specialized high-bandwidth memory (HBM) solutions tailored for the XPU. This collaboration aims to deliver personalized XPUs that leverage the advanced capabilities of the new architecture.

news.headline: Buenos Aires terminates offer to purchase $550M of 7.500% Series 12 Notes due 2027.
news.summary: Buenos Aires, Argentina, December 10, 2024 - The City of Buenos Aires revealed the conclusion of its recent Tender Offer, which aimed to acquire a maximum of $550,000,000 in aggregate principal amount.

news.headline: Eric Trump vows a crypto-friendly President, claiming their embrace after banks cut ties post Jan. 6.
news.summary: During a Middle East conference, the son of President-elect Donald J. Trump revealed that their family has turned to cryptocurrency due to several banks halting their services following the events of January 6th.

news.headline: Judge halts $25B Kroger-Albertsons merger due to FTC concerns about shopper and worker harm.
news.summary: The Federal Trade Commission achieved success in its campaign to prevent the merger of two supermarkets due to worries about negative repercussions on consumers and employees.

news.headline: Ripple approved for NYDFS for RLUSD stablecoin; XRP price up 9%. Analysts predict $2 trillion stablecoin market by 2028.
news.summary: Ripple Labs has recently secured regulatory approval from the New York Department of Financial Services (NYDFS) for its RLUSD stablecoin, a significant milestone in its expansion into the cryptocurrency market. This stablecoin, tied to the US dollar, is a key component in Ripple's efforts to fortify its ecosystem and capitalize on the increasing demand for stablecoins. Analysts have estimated that the stablecoin market could reach a valuation of $2 trillion by 2028. The approval granted by the NYDFS sets the stage for the introduction of RLUSD, with Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse confirming the news on social media platform X (formerly Twitter). Garlinghouse assured that updates regarding RLUSD listings on various exchanges and partner platforms will be communicated by Ripple directly. This development is viewed as a significant step forward for Ripple's strategic objectives in the cryptocurrency realm.

news.headline: Dow Jones falls; Nvidia breaks key level. AI down after earnings. Tesla boosted by analyst upgrade. Inflation data looms.
news.summary: Today, there was a decline in the Dow Jones in the stock market. Nvidia experienced an increase from a significant level of support, while AI stock witnessed a drop following its earnings report. Tesla received a positive momentum thanks to an analyst upgrade. The Dow Jones encountered a setback as Nvidia surpassed a crucial level with the Taiwan Semi report. Inflation data is on the horizon, shaping the market's future.

news.headline: 2025-2030 Dietary Guidelines to prioritize plant-based protein over animal protein; Doctors approve.
news.summary: A recent development in Washington saw the advisory committee responsible for providing scientific guidance on updating the Dietary Guidelines for Americans suggest significant changes for the 2025-2030 revision. It is the first time such a recommendation has been made. The committee advised for the inclusion of more nutrient-dense plant-based meals and dietary options in the upcoming guidelines. They proposed giving priority to plant-based proteins over animal proteins and emphasized the numerous benefits of beans, peas, and lentils as sources of protein. This recommendation comes from the Dietary Guidelines Advisory Committee (DGAC), and signals a potential shift towards more plant-centric dietary recommendations.

news.headline: Trump promises quick approval for investors putting $1B into America.
Karolinska Development sells 4.6 million shares in OssDsign, keeping 5 million; boosts liquidity.
news.summary: In Stockholm, Sweden, as of December 10, 2024, Karolinska Development AB (listed on Nasdaq Stockholm as KDEV) has sold 4.6 million shares in its portfolio firm OssDsign, boosting the investment company's liquidity. Following the divestment, Karolinska Development retains approximately 5 million shares in OssDsign.

news.headline: Nevada enhances blockchain security after fake electors incident, amid the 2024 presidential race aftermath.
news.summary: The timing of the announcement is significant, coming just one month after the conclusion of the 2024 US presidential race.

news.headline: Semiconductor fabrication software market to grow by $984.91 million (2024-2028) due to increased design complexity, AI impact.
news.summary: A recent report analyzing the influence of AI on market trends reveals that the global semiconductor fabrication software market is projected to increase by approximately USD 984.91 million between 2024 and 2028. The market is expected to experience a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of nearly 2.77% during this period.

news.headline: Sycamore Partners in discussions to buy Walgreens; deal involves estimated $70 billion.
HII appoints Jennifer Childs as VP of Quality and Engineering at Ingalls Shipbuilding, starting Jan 6, 2025. Blanchette to lead HII and Ingalls.
news.summary: In Pascagoula, Mississippi, on December 10, 2024, HII revealed that Jennifer Childs is set to become the new vice president of quality and engineering for the Ingalls Shipbuilding division starting January 6, 2025. Childs will take over from Brian Blanchette, who is transitioning to become the vice president of HII and president of Ingalls Shipbuilding.
